title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Cop is a series of action-comedy films produced between 1984 and 2024.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The film series focuses on Axel Foley (Eddie Murphy), a street smart, wisecracking Detriot detective who finds himself investigating cases and murders in Beverly Hills, California.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: He partners with Beverly Hills Police Department officers Detective Billy Rosewood (Judge Reinhold), Sergeant John Taggart (John Ashton), and others during his time in Beverly Hills.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The series started in 1984 with the release of Beverly Hills Cop, which produced two sequels.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The third film was not well received critically, amongst fans, or by Murphy himself.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The three films grossed $735,534,503 worldwide.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: Over twenty years later, Netflix revived the franchise with a fourth film titled, Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: After his childhood buddy is murdered while visiting Detroit, rebellious cop Axel Foley (Eddie Murphy) follows the leads to Beverly Hills, Calif., under the auspices of a vacation.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: He checks in with old friend Jenny Summers (Lisa Eilbacher) and starts to believe her boss, art dealer Victor Maitland (Steven Berkoff), might somehow be involved in the murder.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The hard-nosed Detroit cop from the original film returns to Los Angeles to help solve another case.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: This time he must direct his efforts towards unravelling the Alphabet Crimes, a series of robberies committed by leather-jacketed punks.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The investigation puts him on the trail of an illegal arms dealer and his hit lady.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: When his boss is killed, Detroit cop Alex Foley (Eddie Murphy) finds evidence that the murderer had ties to a California amusement park called Wonder World.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: Returning to Beverly Hills once more, Foley reunites with Detective Billy Rosewood (Judge Reinhold) to solve the case.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: Along with Billy's new partner, Jon Flint (Héctor Elizondo), they discover that the security force of Wonder World is actually part of a counterfeit money operation headed by park manager Orrin Sanderson (John Saxon).

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: Detective Axel Foley (Eddie Murphy) is back on the beat in Beverly Hills.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: After his daughter’s life is threatened, she (Taylour Paige) and Foley team up with a new partner (Joseph Gordon-Levitt) and old pals Billy Rosewood (Judge Reinhold) and John Taggart (John Ashton) to turn up the heat and uncover a conspiracy.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: There is a recurring instrumental theme throughout the film series called "Axel F", which was composed by Harold Faltermeyer.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The theme became popular with audiences, and has been remixed by Crazy Frog.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: A new version of the theme was created for Beverly Hills Cop III, with Faltermeyer not returning for the film.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: The trilogy's soundtracks have been praised, giving rise to such hit songs as "The Heat is On", "Stir it Up" and "Shakedown".

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: A fourth entry in the series was initially announced for release in the mid-1990s, under the production of Eddie Murphy's own production company "Eddie Murphy Productions", though production later fizzled out.

title: Beverly Hills Cop (franchise)
sentence_text: It was re-announced in 2006, when producer Jerry Bruckheimer announced his intention to resurrect the film series, though he eventually gave up his option to produce the film, instead passing production duties to Lorenzo di Bonaventura.

sentence_text: In September 2006 a script, an amalgamation of several earlier drafts, was presented to Murphy who was reported to be "very happy" with the outline which was described as an attempt to recapture the "feel of the original".

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Axel Foley is a fictional character, portrayed by Eddie Murphy, and is the main protagonist of the Beverly Hills Cop film franchise.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Axel was born on August 25, 1957 in Detroit, Michigan.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: He attended Mumford High School and became small-time juvenile hoodlum.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: After a few years in which he was unemployed, Foley joined the Detroit Police Department in 1983.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: A talented policeman, Axel is also known to bend the rules, which annoys his boss, Inspector Todd.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: When Axel and his friend Michael "Mikey" Tandino were younger, they used to get in trouble a lot by stealing cars.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley is delighted when he receives a surprise visit from his best friend Mikey Tandino, who lives in California.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Unfortunately, Mikey is soon killed when Axel was hit in the head, by a man named Zack.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley goes to Beverly Hills, California, to find Mikey's assailant (since Mikey told Foley he had a job at an art gallery in Beverly Hills) where Beverly Hills Police Department Lieutenant Andrew Bogomil assigns Detective Billy Rosewood and Sergeant John Taggart to keep an eye on Foley.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Where he made friends with the two detectives.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley visits his childhood friend Jenny Summers who works at the art gallery owned by a wealthy man named Victor Maitland.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: With Jenny's help, Foley discovers that Zack works for Jenny's boss, Victor Maitland himself.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Maitland is a drug kingpin who is using the gallery as a front, and Maitland ordered Zack to kill Mikey after Maitland accused Mikey of stealing some of Maitland's bonds.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Billy, Taggart, and Foley head to Maitland's mansion to apprehend Maitland.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley and Bogomil simultaneously shoot and kill Maitland.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: In the end he also earned Bogomil's trust.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Before he was about to head back to Detroit, he offered to have a beer with Taggart and Rosewood, even though they are still on duty.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Taggart tells Billy, one beer won't hurt us.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Rosewood said okay, and Axel told the guys he knows a great bar to get a beer.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley is watching the news on TV when he sees a report that his friend, Beverly Hills Police Department Captain Andrew Bogomil, has been shot by a tall, blonde woman and two of her assailants.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley heads out to Beverly Hills to visit Bogomil in the hospital, and this is where Foley is reunited with Bogomil's daughter, Jan Bogomil.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley is also reunited with his friends Detective Billy Rosewood and Sergeant John Taggart.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Rosewood and Taggart decide to let Foley help them find the woman who tried to kill Bogomil, even though abusive police chief Harold Lutz has been deliberately trying to find a reason to fire them.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley, Rosewood, and Taggart soon discover that the alphabet robberies, a series of robberies that have been going on in the area, are masterminded by weapons kingpin Maxwell Dent, and Dent had sent his fiancé Karla Fry to try to kill Bogomil because Bogomil had been after Dent.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: With this information, Foley, Rosewood, and Taggart try to find Dent and Karla.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: They track them down and find out the alphabet crimes is about arms dealing.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Dent and Karla are about to make an arms deal with Nikos Thomopolis in Dent's oil field, framed Charles Cain as the alphabet robber so no one suspect Dent was the mastermind was behind this.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley, Taggart and Rosewood thwarted their plan, took out both Dent and Karla.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Thermopolis and Dent's men are arrested.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: The Mayor fired Lutz for his abusive attitude and jeopardizing the case and commended Foley, Taggart, Rosewood for their service and puts in a good word with Inspector Todd.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: The Mayor promoted Bogomil who survived the gunshot wound and he is the new chief of Police.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: He said goodbye to his friends and headed back home.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Axel is the lead detective for a chop shop investigation and calls off the SWAT officers, believing him and his team can handle a few mechanics.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: The bust goes south and turns into a shoot-out, where Axel witnesses his boss, and friend, Inspector Douglas Todd is murdered.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Axel follows several clues to Wonder World, a theme park in Beverly Hills, California.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: He ignores the orders of Secret Service Agent Steve Fulbright to stand down.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Fulbright insists the man who murdered Todd must remain free as part of a larger federal investigation.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: In the luxurious backdrop of Beverly Hills, Axel is reunited with his old friend, Billy Rosewood, who now holds a prestigious role as Deputy Director of Operations for Joint Systems Interdepartmental Operational Command (DDO-JSIOC).

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Billy, now a big shot, has a new partner, Jonathan "Jon" Flint.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Foley, always one for adventure, decides to explore Wonder World, owned by Dave "Uncle Dave" Thornton.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: While there, Axel springs into action, rescuing two kids stranded on a malfunctioning ride.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: However, his heroics lead him to a startling revelation: Ellis DeWald, the head of security and personal relations, is the very same man who murdered Inspector Todd.

sentence_text: This shocking truth shakes the foundation of their friendship, especially for Flint, who can't believe that Dewald, a friend of his, is running a counterfeiting ring within the theme park.

sentence_text: With the - rather reluctant - help of Billy, Foley sets out to prove his innocence and get revenge on Dewald and Sanderson.

title: Axel Foley
sentence_text: Axel is now a police lieutenant in Detroit whose investigations into crimes against his loved ones often bring him to Beverly Hills.

sentence_text: His latest stunt in foiling a robbery at a hockey game leads the brass to come down hard on Axel's friend and Deputy Chief Jeffrey Friedman.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Lieutenant William "Billy" Rosewood is a fictional character from the Beverly Hills Cop film series, and is portrayed by Judge Reinhold.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Billy was born in 1960 and joined the Beverly Hills Police Department in 1981.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: A good cop, Billy is known to go strictly by the book, and is not as tough as his partner, John Taggart.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: In the first film, he was generally polite and bumbling, and not particularly competent at anything, until it was revealed toward the end of the film that he had excellent marksmanship with a pistol, the second film expands on this weapon proficiency.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He made friends with Axel Foley and sticks up for him from his superiors.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He and his partner John Taggart help Foley take down Victor Maitland who killed Foley's friend Michael Tandino.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: In Beverly Hills Cop II, Rosewood has matured a bit more and come into his own as a detective, perhaps a bit too much due to his newfound obsession with weaponry and Sylvester Stallone, worrying Taggart and Axel enough to suggest numerous times, "Billy, we need to talk." Despite his quirks and longing for excitement, Billy is still a dedicated and good-hearted officer.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Billy has now been promoted to DDO-JSIOC and Detective Sergeant.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Billy is now a big shot.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: When Axel arrives to visit him, he reveals that his former partner, John Taggart, is now retired and living in Arizona.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He also has a new partner in Jon Flint.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He along with Flint refuses to believe that Ellis DeWald is dirty.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: However, he later joins the shootout against DeWald's men.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He is later shot by one of the security guards, but he survives.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He is later seen in a wheelchair aside Axel and Flint as Uncle Dave introduces a new addition to his theme park in Axel's honor, Axel Fox.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Uncle Dave commended Rosewood, Flint and Foley for their service and saving his life.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Several months back, Rosewood made a bust on a Cartel crew out of Adelanto, though the case went nowhere and the charges were dropped for lack of evidence.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: The drugs Rosewood confiscated turned out to be powder.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: The supervisor on the case was Captain Grant, which solidified Rosewood's belief the Captain was dirty.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He accused Grant of swapping out the drugs for the powder, and swore up and down to Chief Taggart that Grant was dirty.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Taggart didn't believe him, so Rosewood turned over his badge.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: This ended their 30 year friendship and Rosewood opened his investigative service, Rosewood Investigations.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Now, when an undercover cop named Lieutenant Copeland is murdered while on duty, the police arrest Sam Enriquez for the murder.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Copeland had been investigating Enriquez's Uncle at the time of his death.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Rosewood thinks Enriquez is being framed by Grant and other dirty cops, so he asks Jane Saunders if anyone in her Law firm can take Enriquez's case.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: She volunteers to take it after reading the file.

sentence_text: After officially joining his case, a group of masked men attack Jane in a parking garage, threatening to kill her if she doesn't drop the case.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: Rosewood visits her after the attack and asks her to leave the case.

title: Billy Rosewood
sentence_text: He doesn't want to be responsible for telling her dad she died.

title: Billy Rosewood

title: Billy Rosewood

title: Beverly Hills Cop

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Victor Maitland is the main antagonist of Beverly Hills Cop.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Victor Maitland was an influential British businessman who owned an art gallery in Beverly Hills which was really a front for a large-scale smuggling operation.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: His organization's modus operandi involved bribing key customs officers to separate deliveries for his gallery, which were then transported to one of his warehouses, where the contraband - either foreign bearer bonds or drugs - would be removed and the shipment returned to the federal customs office.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Maitland eventually hired Jenny Summers as the manager of his gallery, and upon her request he also hired one of her childhood friends, Michael Tandino, as a security guard for the warehouse.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: One day, just before he went to visit his and Jenny's mutual old friend, Axel Foley, in Detroit, Tandino chanced upon an opened shipment crate left unattended, and grabbed some of the bearer bonds inside for himself.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: But the theft did not go unnoticed; Maitland sent his chief henchman, Zack, after Tandino.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: They caught up with him right in front of Foley's apartment, and after retrieving the stolen bonds, Zack personally killed Tandino by shooting him through the back of the head.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Foley, who had been knocked unconscious during the deed, decided to investigate Tandino's murder and travelled to Beverly Hills, where he first visited Summers to inform her of Tandino's death and then went to Maitland to question him about Tandino's activities.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Confronted with Foley's snooping, Maitland had Foley forcibly evicted from his office and arrested for disturbance of peace, but this only served to pique Foley's interest even more.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Eventually Foley came behind Maitland's clandestine operation, but at first lacked solid evidence to convince his new allies at the Beverly Hills Police Department, Lieutenant Bogomil, Sergeant Taggart and Detective Rosewood, of Maitland's criminality.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Maitland hunting Foley in his mansion

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: After winning Rosewood's friendship, Foley eventually uncovered a cache of recently arrived drugs stashed at Maitland's warehouse.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: However, Maitland had anticipated this action and took Foley and Summers, who had entered the warehouse without Rosewood, prisoner.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: After taking Summers with him for possible torture and execution, Maitland retreated to his mansion at 609 Palm Canyon Road, leaving Axel to be beaten to death by his bodyguards.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Thanks to Rosewood's timely intervention, Foley managed to escape, and the two officers, soon joined by Taggart, prepared to enter the Maitland mansion and free Summers from captivity.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Maitland meets his demise

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: After engaging some of Maitland's thugs in a series of firefights, Foley managed to penetrate the mansion and kill Zack, but was right afterwards wounded by Maitland.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Before Maitland could finish him off, Foley managed to get away, forcing Maitland to take Summers as a hostage for his escape.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: However, Foley and company's activities on the mansion ground had alarmed Bogomil, who swiftly proceeded to the mansion with a large police relief force.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: Bogomil's arrival to back up Foley managed to catch Maitland off-guard.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: After Summers managed to use this distraction to slip out of his clutches, Maitland fired at Foley, but his shot missed.

title: Victor Maitland
sentence_text: In turn, Foley and Bogomil opened fire on Maitland, who was killed in a hail of bullets.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Ellis DeWald is the main antagonist of Beverly Hills Cop III.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Ellis DeWald was a cop in an unknown police department, but somewhere along the line, he left and became corrupt, using his knowledge for evil purposes.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: He became the head of security for a famous theme park named Wonder World and used it as the perfect cover-up to manufacture counterfeit dollar bills and smuggle them around the country with the help of his accomplices, corrupt park manager Orrin Sanderson and also corrupt Secret Service agent Steve Fulbright.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: He also became friends with unsuspecting Beverly Hills police officer Jon Flint, Billy Rosewood's new partner.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: It is also implied that he killed park designer and close friend of Wonder World creator Uncle Dave Thornton, Roger Fry, after he found out about his scheme.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald went with a few of his men, including his head henchman Holloway to Detroit, Michigan, to an illegal chop shop to acquire a van filled with mint paper that was used to make the notes.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: After being shown the van and its contents, he double-crossed the workmen, who had been promised payment for their service, by ordering his men to shoot them all in cold blood to leave no witnesses.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: However, when a team of police officers led by Axel Foley who had come to the chop shop to arrest the workman got involved, a massive shootout took place resulting in one of the men being shot and in turn DeWald shooting police chief Douglas Todd who was also Axel's boss.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Axel saw his face and chased after him after some encouragement from his boss with no success thanks to DeWald tipping off Fulbright to stop him.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Axel then followed him to Wonder World after his men had carelessly left behind evidence of Wonder World engraved towels.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald sent his men to capture him, but Axel escaped.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: After a frightening experience on the malfunctioning Spider ride, in which Axel saved two children, DeWald's men caught him and brought him to the corrupt businessman.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: The cop recognized him at once, but Jon Flint who had just arrived, refused to believe Axel's story since they were old friends.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Axel has led away and DeWald told Flint to keep Axel away from the park.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Determined to prove that DeWald was a liar, Axel decided to turn up at his Man of the Year celebration dinner where he was receiving an award for community service to trick him into revealing his true colors.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Along with Billy Rosewood, Axel arrived and openly went up on stage and revealed in front of the crowd that he would pay back DeWald everything that was owed to him with interest (cleverly disguising the fact that he was really going to avenge Douglas Todd).

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald in turn as they both began putting on false smiles, replied: "What's the matter Foley?

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: You mad at me for shooting your boyfriend?" Hearing this, Axel punched DeWald in the face in a rage before being dragged away by DeWald's men and arrested along with Rosewood for disturbing the peace.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Knowing Axel was now onto him, DeWald put all his men on alert to capture Axel if they should see him.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: However Axel disguised himself as one of the Wonder World mascots Okey Dokey and infiltrated the main room where the counterfeit operation was taking place.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald however, noticed Axel's reflection in one of the glass lights and sent his men to capture him, but Axel once again escaped.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald's men chased him and not wanting to put anyone in danger, Axel fired his gun in the air, placed it on the ground and surrendered.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald's men then took him away and Axel tried to convince Rosewood and Flint about the counterfeiting.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald and Sanderson willingly took them to the room, but had craftily hidden the counterfeit bills and replaced them with currency that would be used for the theme park, so once again Axel's story was unconvincing.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald later found out that he had contacted Uncle Dave and that Roger Fry had left behind some evidence that linked him to his crimes.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: It seemed like a blank note with only the words: "This is important, life and death.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Roger." However it turned out to be a piece of mint paper that Roger had apparently managed to retrieve before his death and had given to Uncle Dave as well as another employee named Janice Perkins who was helping Axel with the case.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald then shot Uncle Dave with Axel's own gun that he had confiscated from him earlier and proceeded to take the mint paper away from him, but Axel managed to hold DeWald at gunpoint in time to snatch back the mint paper and drive Uncle Dave to hospital.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: DeWald then framed Axel for Uncle Dave's attempted murder by broadcasting it on the news, then returned to the park where he took Janice hostage.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: When Axel called, DeWald told him to meet him at the park, alone and unarmed.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: His terms were papier-mâché for Janice's life.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Axel agreed and, after arriving, was taken to the control room to see him.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: When Axel refused to hand over the mint paper, DeWald threatened to kill them both, rendering the paper useless.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Declaring to Sanderson, who was holding Janice, that he knows how cops think, he threatened Axel with the car keys because he knew the note was hidden in the car.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Axel, however, brandished a key ring and pressed a button that emitted a blinding flash, stunning DeWald long enough for him to grab his gun and tell Holloway to drop it.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: Suddenly, Rosewood, who had been warned in advance of Axel's intentions, entered the room and a huge fight and gunfight ensued.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: After being warned by Sanderson that Axel was using an outside line to get help, DeWald cut the phone lines, leaving Axel to fend for himself.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: He then sent a warning message to Foley before he and Holloway set off in pursuit of the cop.

title: Ellis DeWald
sentence_text: After more of his men were killed by Axel, Rosewood and Flint (who was informed by a fireman that Axel had killed Uncle Dave without knowing that DeWald was the real culprit), DeWald and Holloway followed Axel to the Land of the Dinosaurs attraction.

title: Ellis DeWald

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: West German Browning Hi-Power.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Browning Hi Power is a single-action, semi-automatic handgun available in 9mm and .40 S&W calibers.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: It is Axel Foley's main sidearm.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: It is based on a design by American firearms inventor John Browning, and completed by Dieudonné Saive at Fabrique Nationale (FN) of Herstal, Belgium.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: Browning died in 1926, several years before the design was finalized.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Hi-Power is one of the most widely used military pistols in history, having been used by the armed forces of over 50 countries.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Hi Power name is somewhat misleading and alluded to the 13-round magazine capacity; almost twice that of contemporary designs such as the Luger P08 or Mauser 1910.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Browning was one of the first, if not the first, pistols to utilize a double-stack magazine with ten or thirteen round capacities, thus making it more high-powered than pistols with single-stack magazines, like the 1911, which is what the Hi-Power is partly based off of.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: However, despite the Hi-Power being a single-action firearm, it had a heavy trigger pull, almost ten pounds, which is far heavier than that of the 1911, which was less than four pounds.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The pistol is often referred to as an HP (for "Hi-Power" or "High-Power") or as a GP (for the French term, "Grande Puissance").

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The terms P-35 and HP-35 are also used, based on the introduction of the pistol in 1935.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: It is most often called the "Hi Power", even in Belgium.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: Other names include BAP (Browning Automatic Pistol), particularly in Irish service, or BHP (Browning High-Power).

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Police Department Captain Andrew Bogomil, Detective Billy Rosewood, and Sergeant John Taggart are trying to figure out who is behind the "Alphabet Crimes," a series of mostly high end store robberies distinguished by their monogrammed envelopes with an alphabetical sequence the assailants leave behind.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Complicating matters is the new "political" state of the Beverly Hills Police Department, headed by incompetent, egotistical, and verbally abusive new police chief Harold Lutz, who is doing everything he can to stay on Mayor Ted Egan's good side.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Unimpressed when Rosewood calls the FBI to help solve the case, Lutz holds Bogomil responsible as commanding officer and suspends him, despite Bogomil's efforts to convince the chief that Rosewood was only following a hunch.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Lutz also punishes Taggart and Rosewood by placing them on traffic duty.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: On the way home, Bogomil is shot and injured by Karla Fry, the chief henchwoman and fiancé of Maxwell Dent.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Finding out about the shooting over a news report, Detroit Police Department detective Axel Foley abandons his undercover duties and immediately flies out to Beverly Hills to help find out who shot Bogomil.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Taggart and Rosewood agree to assist Axel because of Lutz's attempts to find an excuse to get them fired.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Posing as an undercover FBI agent to get past Lutz with the aid of Detective Jeffrey Friedman, Axel soon starts making the connection between the robberies and Dent.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: He first finds out that the ammunition fired at one of the robberies was designed by the manager of a gun club owned by Dent.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Axel has Bogomil's daughter Jan use her connections as an insurance agent to find out about Dent's financial dealings.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Dent is robbing his own businesses on purpose in order to finance firearms transactions with an arms dealer named Nikos Thomopolis and is discreetly using his henchman Charles Cain as the front man for his operations.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Bogomil was shot because his investigation was on the correct track into the case.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Having foiled a robbery attempt at a bank depot, Axel is able to trick Dent's accountant Sidney Bernstein into using his computer and discovers that Dent and Karla are planning to leave the country.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Axel also learns from Jan that all of Dent's businesses have had their insurance coverage canceled and are about to go bankrupt except his race track, which he is convinced is the next target.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: On the way to the race track, Axel solves the latest riddle sent to the police, and is convinced that this riddle was made easily solvable in order to implicate Cain as the Alphabet Bandit.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: However, Axel knows Cain is a patsy designed to throw the authorities off Dent's trail.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: The three arrive too late to stop the robbery and find Cain's body among those killed.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: While Lutz announces publicly that the Alphabet Crimes have been solved, Axel notices some red mud at the stables, which leads him, Taggart and Rosewood to Dent's oil field, where Dent is making his final arms deal with Thomopolis.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: The three get into a shootout with everyone involved in the deal.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Dent confronts Axel in the warehouse, but Axel gets distracted by one of Dent's henchmen on the roof above him and Dent gets away.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Dent then crashes through the wall in his car and Axel shoots Dent through the windshield, sending his car down a hill and erupting in flames, after running Axel over.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Karla appears and is about to kill Axel, but is shot dead by Taggart.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Just as the last criminals are about to flee, the police arrive upon the scene, arresting the remainder of Dent's men and Thomopolis.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: After Lutz and Mayor Egan arrive, an infuriated Lutz tries to fire Rosewood and Taggart for their insubordination, and also tries to arrest Axel.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: However, both Taggart and Rosewood stand up to Lutz this time and prove that Dent was the real Alphabet Bandit and that the alphabet crimes were about an arms deal.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: They are also able to convince Mayor Egan of Lutz's incompetence (as Lutz would have interfered if he had known about their activities, as he intentionally ignores the truth from other cops), and Mayor Egan personally fires Lutz because he is tired of his "abusive attitude" towards his own men and for jeopardizing the investigation

sentence_text: Axel returns to Detroit, but not before he gets chewed out by Inspector Todd over the phone, after Egan called Todd to congratulate him on allowing Axel to assist them on this case.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Cop II was one of the most anticipated films of 1987 and became a box office success upon release, despite not making as much as Beverly Hills Cop.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: The film debuted at No. 1, earning $33 million on its opening weekend, a sales mark that would result in its being that year's highest-opening weekend debut.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Cop II made approximately $153,665,036, becoming the third biggest hit domestically at the box office that year, after Fatal Attraction and Three Men and a Baby, and the second highest-grossing film worldwide that year, behind Fatal Attraction.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: The film received a mixed reception.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Rotten Tomatoes, a review aggregator, reports that 46% of 26 surveyed critics gave it a positive review; the average rating is 4.9/10.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Metacritic, which assigns a normalized score, rated it 48/100 based on 11 reviews.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Desson Howe of The Washington Post called it "a sequel that's as good as the original, if not better." Roger Ebert rated it 1/4 stars and wrote, "What is comedy?

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: That's a pretty basic question, I know, but Beverly Hills Cop II never thought to ask it." Janet Maslin of The New York Times wrote that it is skillful clone of the first film that can't match that one's novelty or excitement.

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: Variety called it "a noisy, numbing, unimaginative, heartless remake of the original film." Sheila Benson of the Los Angeles Times wrote, "It's hard to believe that the group who came up with the hard, clean edges of Top Gun, sleek and unfeeling though it may have been, could make a picture as crude, as muddled, as destructo-Derbyish as this one."

title: Beverly Hills Cop II
sentence_text: This is the only film in the Beverly Hills Cop franchise in which the villain was a woman.

title: Beverly Hills Cop III
sentence_text: One night in Detroit, Axel Foley (Eddie Murphy) plans an invasion at a nearby local chop shop and in turn, cancels the SWAT, enraging his boss Inspector G.

sentence_text: Foley pulls Todd into cover, where he tells the Detective to go get his killer, before he dies.

sentence_text: He kills the man's henchman, but he gets away when he is intercepted by Agent Steve Fulbright, who informs him that he must not apprehend the killer at this time for their investigation on him.

title: Beverly Hills Cop III
sentence_text: After Todd's funeral, Axel learns that the killer's vehicle was rented using a credit card reported stolen from a man on vacation at Wonder World, a theme park in Beverly Hills, California.

title: Beverly Hills Cop III
sentence_text: Axel arrives in Beverly Hills and reunites with his friend Billy Rosewood (Judge Reinhold), who has been promoted to "Deputy Director of Operations for Joint Systems Interdepartmental Operational Command" (DDO-JSIOC).

sentence_text: Axel meets Billy's new partner, Jon Flint (Héctor Elizondo), who claims that he has heard stories about him from John Taggart (John Ashton).

sentence_text: Axel investigates Wonder World, which is owned by "Uncle" Dave Thorton (Alan Young).

sentence_text: Axel meets and befriends Janice Perkins (Theresa Randle) whilst investigating.

sentence_text: The guards accidentally jam the ride, placing two little kids' lives in danger.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Gen. 3 Glock 17 (the one in the movie was a Gen. 2)

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock 17 is an Austrian striker-fired semi-automatic pistol chambered in 9x19mm.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It is used by police and armies around the world and is famous for its 17 round polymer-coated magazine.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It was used in Beverly Hills Cop III as Ellis DeWald's main weapon.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock 17 was introduced in 1982.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Its inventor, Gaston Glock, was an Austrian engineer who only had experience with synthetic polymers, not firearms.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: He later got the idea to make a simply constructed pistol almost entirely out of polymers to compete for the spot in the Austrian military pistol trials to replace the aging Walther P38.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: However, the Glock 17 is not the first polymer handgun.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Heckler and Koch of Germany twenty years earlier made the VP70 to little success.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glock got together a team of firearms experts from across Europe to determine the most desirable traits in a handgun, designed around the criteria, which included that all actions necessary to prepare the pistol for firing must be done single-handed or ambidextrous, disassembly of the main parts must not require tools, the pistol's construction may not exceed 58 individual parts (equivalent of a P38, although Glocks have 34) and after firing 15,000 rounds of standard ammunition, the pistol will fire an overpressure test cartridge.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock's frame, magazine body and several other components are made from a high-strength nylon-based radio-opaque polymer invented by Gaston Glock and called Polymer 2.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: This plastic was specially formulated to provide increased durability and is more resilient than carbon steel and most steel alloys.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Polymer 2 is resistant to shock, caustic liquids and temperature extremes where traditional steel/alloy frames would warp and become brittle.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The injection molded frame's trigger guard is squared off at the front and checkered.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The grip has a non-slip, stippled surface checkering on the sides and both the front and rear straps.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock 17 outperformed 8 different pistols from five other established manufacturers (Heckler & Koch of Germany offered their P7M8, P7M13 and P9S, SIG Sauer of Germany bid with their P220 and P226 models, Beretta of Italy submitted their model 92SB-F, FN Herstal proposed an updated variant of the Browning Hi-Power and the home-grown Steyr entered the competition with the GB).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glock later emerged as the winner of all 17 of the grueling endurance tests and was quickly adopted into service with the Austrian military and police forces in 1982 as the P80 (Pistole 80), with an initial order for 25,000 guns.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Although the Glock Model 17 is noted for its 17-round magazine, that is not why it is called the Glock 17.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It is so named because the final design of the Glock 17 was Gaston Glock's seventeenth patent.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Oddly enough, the Glock 17 also met all 17 requirements for the Austrian military trials.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Although the Glock was mocked for its blocky appearance, sights and controls, lack of hammer, lack of external safety and polymer parts and for years there was a bias against polymer guns on the market due to fears that they would not be detected in metal detectors.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: This fear was later proven to be untrue but nonetheless was featured in Die Hard 2 (which just so happened to be the first movie of many to feature a Glock).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The results of the Austrian trials sparked interest in the United States, where a similar effort to select a service-wide replacement for the M1911 had been ongoing since the late 1970s (known as the Joint Service Small Arms Program).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: In late 1983, the United States Department of Defense inquired about the Glock pistol and received four samples of the Glock 17 for unofficial evaluation.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glock was then invited to participate in the XM9 Personal Defense Pistol Trials, but declined because the DOD specifications would require extensive retooling of production equipment and providing 35 test samples in an unrealistic time frame.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Shortly thereafter, the Glock 17 was accepted into service with the Norwegian, Swiss, German, French, Russian, British and Swedish Armed Forces, surpassing all prior NATO durability standards.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: By 1992, some 350,000 pistols had been sold in more than 45 countries, including 250,000 in the United States alone.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Today it is one of the easiest pistols to disassemble and the Glock is unique in the way that the milled slides can easily be switched from pistol to pistol to allow for different caliber changes.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It is also the only pistol that has three separate internal safeties that engage at once.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock 17 is one of the only three Glock models to be manufactured in all four generations.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: There are three safeties on the gun (trigger safety, drop safety and firing pin block), and since it is technically a light double action trigger as opposed to a hammer-fired designation such as DA/SA, the Glock is known as the "safe action pistol".

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glocks today are used by sport shooters, law enforcement, numerous armies across the globe and civilian defense.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Over 7 million Glocks have been produced as of 2007.

title: Glock 17

title: Beretta 92F

sentence_text: The gun has an effective range of fifty meters and a muzzle velocity of 1,250 feet per second.

sentence_text: One of the most interesting and unique features of the Beretta 92 is its exposed barrel, which allows for less of a chance of a jam.

title: Beretta 92F
sentence_text: Other pistols like the Glock or 1911, have a hidden barrel, cloaked underneath the side, with the ejection port on one side of the gun.

title: Beretta 92F

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Weighing over four pounds and almost a foot in length, the Desert Eagle was designed in America as early as 1979 yet produced in Israel by IMI, creator of the Uzi submachine gun.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: The Desert Eagle was originally designed to pierce an engine block if there was a bomb on the vehicle but due to its large size and expensive price, it was never actually adopted by a military outside of Israel's IDF, despite common media perception.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: The Desert Eagle is a semi-automatic pistol that relies on a gas-operation mechanism usually found on assault rifles like the AK-47, with a light single-action trigger similar to the 1911 and a slide-mounted safety, similar to the Beretta 92FS.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: The Desert Eagle is fed with a detachable magazine.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Magazine capacity is 9 rounds in .357 Magnum, 8 rounds in .44 Magnum, and 7 rounds in .50 Action Express.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: The Desert Eagle's barrel features polygonal rifling.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: The advantage of the gas operation is that it allows the use of far more powerful cartridges than traditional semi-automatic pistol designs.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Thus it allows the Desert Eagle to compete in an area that had previously been dominated by magnum revolvers.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Downsides of the gas-operated mechanism are the large size of the Desert Eagle, and the fact that it discourages the use of unjacketed lead bullets, as lead particles sheared off during firing could clog the gas release tap, preventing proper function.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: It also features a triangular muzzle, which helps with accuracy.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Another downside to a large-caliber automatic is the size of the grip and magazines.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: As a result, it allows for small magazine capacity and almost completely eliminates users with medium to small hands.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: However, gas operation allows for much lower recoil than traditional revolvers, also being able to incorporate more powerful revolver or wildcat cartridges not seen in revolver platforms previously.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: It also allows for larger capacity than a revolver, but not by much.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Switching a Mark XIX Desert Eagle to another chambering requires only that the correct barrel, bolt assembly, and magazine be installed.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: Thus, a conversion to fire the other cartridges can be quickly accomplished.

title: Desert Eagle Mark I
sentence_text: The rim diameter of the .50 AE (Action Express) is the same as the .44 Remington Magnum cartridge, consequently only a barrel and magazine change is required to convert a .44 Desert Eagle to the larger, more powerful .50 AE round.

sentence_text: The most popular barrel length is 6 in (152 mm), although a 10 in (254 mm) barrel is available.

sentence_text: The Mark XIX barrels are machined with integral scope mounting bases, simplifying the process of adding a pistol scope, although few actually put more weight on a four pound handgun.

sentence_text: Barrel lengths were 6, 10 and 14 inches for .357 Magnum and .44 Magnum, but only 6 or 10 inches for .41 Magnum.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Karla Fry is the secondary antagonist in Beverly Hills Cop II.

sentence_text: She was an experienced shooter, expensive gun collector, skilled thief, remorseless killer and master of disguise.

sentence_text: Not much is known about her past, but she works for Maxwell Dent.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Police Captain Andrew Bogomil, Detective Billy Rosewood and Sergeant John Taggart are trying to figure out who is behind the "Alphabet Crimes," a series of mostly high end store robberies distinguished by their monogrammed envelopes with an alphabetical sequence the assailants leave behind.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Complicating matters is the new "political" state of the Beverly Hills Police Department, headed by an incompetent and verbally abusive new police chief Harold Lutz, who is doing everything he can to stay on Mayor Ted Egan's good side.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Unimpressed when Rosewood calls the FBI to help solve the case, Lutz holds Bogomil responsible as commanding officer and suspends him, despite Bogomil's efforts to convince him that Rosewood was only following a hunch, a traditional aspect of police work.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Lutz also punishes Taggart and Rosewood by placing them on traffic duty.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: On the way home, Bogomil is shot and injured by Karla, because she is the chief hench-woman of Maxwell Dent.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Finding out about the shooting by a news report, Axel Foley immediately flies out to Beverly Hills (covering his absence from his actual job in Detroit by telling his commanding officer Inspector Todd he is going "deep deep deep DEEP undercover" on the credit card fraud case he had been assigned to) to help find out who shot Bogomil, to repay the favor he owes Bogomil for saving his job two years ago.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Taggart and Rosewood agree to assist Foley because of Lutz's apparent attempts to find an excuse to get them fired.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Posing as an undercover FBI agent to get past Lutz (by getting his would-be partner Jeffrey Friedman in Detroit to pose as Todd, and intercept Lutz's phone call to Todd's office, to convince Lutz that Foley is part of a multi-jurisdictional task force), Foley soon starts making the connection between the robberies and Dent.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Foley has Bogomil's daughter Jan use her connections as an insurance agent to find out about Dent's financial dealings: Dent is robbing his own businesses on purpose in order to finance firearms deals and is discreetly using his henchman Charles Cain as the front man for his operations.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Bogomil was shot because his investigation was on the correct track into the case.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: "What does this mean to you, Andrew?"

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Having foiled a robbery attempt at a bank depot, Foley is able to trick Dent's accountant Sidney Bernstein into using his computer and discovers that Dent and Karla are planning to leave the country.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Foley also learns from Jan that all of Dent's businesses have had their insurance coverage canceled and are about to go bankrupt except his race track, which he is convinced is the next target.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: On the way to the race track, Foley solves the latest riddle sent to the police, and is convinced that this riddle was made easily solvable in order to implicate Cain as the Alphabet Bandit, although Foley, after meeting him earlier, knows Cain is a patsy designed to throw the authorities off of Dent's trail.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: The three arrive too late to stop the robbery and find Cain's body (he was shot by Karla) among those killed.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: While Lutz announces publicly that the Alphabet Crimes have been solved, Foley notices some red mud at the stables, which leads him, Taggart and Rosewood to Dent's oil field, where Dent is making his final arms deal.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: The three get into a shootout with everyone involved in the deal.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Dent confronts Foley in the warehouse, but Foley gets distracted by one of Dent's henchmen on the roof above him and Dent gets away.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Dent then crashes through the wall in his car and Foley shoots Dent through the windshield, sending his car down a hill and erupting in flames, after running Foley over.

title: Karla Fry
sentence_text: Karla appears and is about to kill Foley with her Desert Eagle, as a last act for revenge for killing Maxwell Dent, but is shot dead by Taggart.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: Maxwell Dent is the main antagonist of Beverly Hills Cop II.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: Much of Maxwell Dent's background is unknown, but it is indicated that he was an East German in origin; in the Seventies he served as a cultural attaché for the East German embassy in Honduras.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: He eventually retired to Beverly Hills, California with the money he had accumulated, and established himself as a respectable business man majoring in oil production.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: However, in time Dent's businesses failed and he faced total bankruptcy.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: In order to cut his losses and recuperate his fortune, Dent struck a deal with arms dealer Nikos Thomopolis to purchase and ship a large delivery of weapons to his old political contacts in Central America.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: To procure the money for the deal, he established a complicated crime scheme, assigning Karla, his loyal assistant and lover, and Chip Cain, the managers of Dent's Beverly Hills gun club, as his task leaders, and selecting financially high-profile targets across Beverly Hills.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: The trademark calling card of these so-called "Alphabet Crimes" was an encoded letter left at the crime scenes.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: However, Captain Andrew Bogomil of the Beverly Hills Police Department somehow caught wind of Dent's criminal intentions.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: But Bogomil's investigations did not go unnoticed, and Dent had him targeted for assassination. (Ironically, just before the attempt on his life, Bogomil and his loyal underlings John Taggart and Billy Rosewood had just been suspended by their incompetent new chief Harold Lutz for widening the investigation to a federal level, while Lutz intended to keep the matter small in order not to incur the displeasure of mayor Ted Egan.) When he heard of the attempted murder, Axel Foley, a close friend of the three officers, immediately proceeded to Beverly Hills to investigate.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: Within a short time, Foley singled out Dent as the prime suspect and, with assistance from Taggart and Rosewood, even managed to foil one major robbery on the City Deposit.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: Still, thanks to Karla's tireless work, the initial phase was finished on schedule, and in order to throw the police off his track, Dent had Cain killed by Karla as a stand-in patsy.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: However, even though Lutz was quick to announce the end of the Alphabet Crimes, Foley and company followed another clue down to Dent's oil fields, where the last stage of the arms deal was already underway.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: Owing to a misguided attempt at stealth by Rosewood, the alarm was raised, and Foley, Rosewood and Taggart were forced to engage Dent's and Thermopolis' henchmen in a firefight.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: With the arrival of the police, Dent sought revenge on Foley for foiling him by attempting to run him over with his car.

title: Maxwell Dent
sentence_text: Foley, however, was able to shoot Dent in the head through the car's windshield; the car subsequently plunged down a cliffside and exploded upon hitting the ground, incinerating his body.

sentence_text: Some other features of the gun is the 'no fire' without a magazine and the 'safe' half cock mode (if you pull the trigger lightly, the hammer will go half way back and lock) and de-cocking lever/safety on the slide.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Smith and Wesson Model 39 was a first-generation semi-automatic pistol developed for the US Army service pistol trials of 1954.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: After the Army abandoned its search for a new pistol, the Model 39 went on the civilian market in 1955 and was the first of Smith & Wesson's first generation semi-automatic pistols.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: Used by the Illinois State Police and the US Navy SEALs, it was a groundbreaking pistol for use with the military and law-enforcement agencies.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The S&W 639 (the stainless, second-generation version of the S&W 39) was seen in Beverly Hills Cop.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Smith and Wesson Model 39 was the first U.S. designed double action (DA) semi-automatic pistol marketed in the United States.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The German Walther P38 DA pistol impressed American ordnance personnel during World War II.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Army Ordnance Corps issued a proposal for an American equivalent to the P38.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: In 1949, Smith and Wesson began development of the Model 39 DA semi-auto in caliber 9x19mm Luger.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: Smith and Wesson began selling the Model 39 commercially in 1955.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: This is considered a first generation pistol.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: Since the Model 39 came out, S&W has continuously developed the design into its third generation pistols now on the market.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: First generation models use a 2 digit model number, second generation use 3 digits, and third generation models use 4 digits.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was originally manufactured with an anodized aluminum frame, a curved backstrap and a blued carbon steel slide that carried the manual safety.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The grip was of three pieces made of two walnut wood panels joined by a metal backstrap.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: It has a magazine release located at the rear of the trigger guard, similar to the M1911A1 it was designed to replace.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was produced in limited numbers with a steel frame.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: This steel-frame model provided the basis for the Model 52, one of the outstanding target pistols of the day.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 52 had a longer (5 inch) barrel and slide and was one of the few semi-automatic pistols ever chambered for the .38 Special cartridge (with flush-seated, full wadcutter bullets only).

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The shape of the rimmed cartridge limited the magazine capacity to five rounds.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: A further variant, the Model 952, in 9mm Para, is still produced in limited quantities by Smith & Wesson's Performance Center.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 52 was discontinued in 1992 when the machinery to manufacture the pistol broke down and it was deemed too costly to replace.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was the basis for the later Smith & Wesson Model 59, retaining the original 9mm Parabellum caliber, but incorporating a wider aluminum frame with a straight backstrap to accommodate a double-column magazine that could hold 14 rounds.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 used a conventional slide, barrel bushing, slide arrangement as opposed to the exposed barrel arrangement of the P38.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: Model 39 locking is done by a modification of the Browning P35 (High Power) cam-locked breech.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: First generation Model 39 slides used either a long, spring steel extractor or a spring-loaded, pivoting claw extractor.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: Long extractors tended to be fragile and were replaced by the pivoting type.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: However, the long extractor proved to be more reliable than the pivoting version.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 employed many features common to the Walther P38 such as a decocking safety that disconnected the trigger and hammer.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: Smith & Wesson even copied the 8-round single-stack magazine as well but added a magazine catch cutout to it.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The overall length of the Model 39 was 7.6 inches, the barrel is 4 inches long.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The weight of the Model 39 was 1.72 pounds; this light weight is due to its aluminum frame.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Illinois State Police adopted the Model 39 in 1967, an action which helped ingratiate semi-automatic pistols with law enforcement.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: This publicity helped commercial sales and set the stage for the more acceptable Model 59 with its high capacity magazine at least in undercover or detective police use where the DA feature (and the larger magazine capacity) was considered by many to be superior to single-stack semi-automatics and revolvers of the time.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: A modified version of he Model 39 with a wider grip frame to accommodate a 14-round magazine was used by Naval Special Warfare units during the Vietnam War.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: It was issued to officers-in-charge (OICs) of MST-2 detachments as their sidearm.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: SEAL Teams used either the Model 39 without modification or a modified version, the Mk 22 Mod 0, which was called the "hush puppy".

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The modified pistol had a suppressor with a slide lock (which kept the slide from moving backwards upon firing, thereby decreasing produced noise) The Mk 22 Mod 0 had raised iron sights, to provide easier sighting over a bulky suppressor.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The gun's purpose was to eliminate sentry dogs or guards without alarming the main target.

title: Smith \u0026 Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was later modified into the ASP-9 pistol.

title: Colt Python
sentence_text: The Colt Python is a .357 Magnum caliber revolver seen within the Beverly Hills Cop franchise.

title: Colt Python
sentence_text: The Colt Python is a double action handgun chambered for the .357 Magnum cartridge, built on Colt's large I-frame.

title: Colt Python
sentence_text: Pythons have a reputation for accuracy, smooth trigger pull, and a tight cylinder lock-up.

title: Colt Python
sentence_text: They are similar in size and function to the Colt Trooper and Colt Lawman revolvers.

title: Colt Python
sentence_text: Although Pythons were very expensive due to their quality, many police officers preferred to carry the Python because of its fine trigger pull.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: West German P226.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The SIG-Sauer P226 is a full-sized Swiss service pistol that was first produced in 1984.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: It is chambered for multiple calibers, including 9mm, .357 SIG, .40 S&W and .22 LR.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: It was seen in Beverly Hills Cop III.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The P226 was originally designed as a double-stack 9mm version of the SIG-Sauer P220 for entry into the XM9 Service Pistol Trials (see also Joint Service Small Arms Program) that were held by the US Army in 1984 on behalf of the US armed forces to find a replacement for the M1911A1.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Only the Beretta 92F and the SIG P226 satisfactorily completed the trials.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: According to a GAO report, Beretta was awarded the M9 contract for the 92F due to a lower total package price.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The P226 cost less per pistol than the 92F, but SIG's package price with magazines and spare parts was higher than Beretta's.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The Navy SEALs, however, chose to adopt the P226 later.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Schweizerische Industrie Gesellschaft is a Swiss company and Swiss law severely restricts the export of firearms.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Consequently, SIG entered into an agreement with German gun manufacturer (and eventual owner) J.P.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Sauer & Sohn to facilitate an export market for their products.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: For the U.S. military XM9 trials, the P226 was imported by Saco Defense.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Interarms took over importing when the pistol was introduced for civilian sales.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: SIG Sauer eventually founded SIGARMS, Inc. in the United States, to handle importation of their products.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: In 2000 the SIG Holding AG sold J.P.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Sauer & Sohn GmbH to two German businessmen.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The brand name SIG Sauer remained at the J.P.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Sauer & Sohn GmbH.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: It was developed to use higher capacity, staggered-column double stack magazines in place of the single-column magazines of the P220.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The P226 itself has spawned further sub-variants; the P228 and P229 are both compact versions of the staggered-column P226 design.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The SIG Sauer P226 and its variants are in service with numerous law enforcement and military organizations worldwide, including the Navy SEALs.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The all-metal P226, like the other members of the SIG Classic family, operates by the locked breech short-recoil method pioneered by John Browning.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: On firing, the slide and barrel are locked together for a few millimeters of rearward movement, after which the barrel is cammed down at the rear.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: By this time the bullet has left the barrel and the pressure has dropped to safe levels, whereupon the slide completes the rearward stroke, ejecting the spent cartridge.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The recoil spring then propels the slide forward, stripping a round from the magazine and in the last few millimeters of forward movement the barrel is cammed upwards, locking the slide and barrel together again.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Instead of the locking lugs and recesses milled into the barrel and slide of other Browning-type weapons (such as the Colt M1911A1, Browning Hi-Power and CZ 75), the P226 locks the barrel and slide together using an enlarged breech section of the barrel locking into the ejection port.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: This modified system, which was devised by SIG based on Charles Petter's Modèle 1935A pistol and their own SIG P210, has no functional disadvantages compared to the original system, and has since been copied by numerous firearm manufacturers.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The slide of the pre-1996 P226 was a heavy gauge, mill finished sheet metal stamping with a welded on nose section incorporating an internal barrel bushing.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The breech block portion was a machined insert attached to the slide by means of brazing and a roll pin visible from either side.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Since 1996, production has shifted to CNC machining and the slide is now milled from a single piece of stainless steel.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Therefore the current standard P226 has a black anodized, stainless steel slide.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: This resulted in a stronger slide, which was necessary to chamber the more powerful .40 S&W and .357 SIG cartridges.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The frame of most models is made from hard anodized aluminum alloy.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The standard SIG P226 incorporates a decocking lever on the left side of the frame above the magazine release button, which first appeared on the Sauer 38H prior to World War II, which allows the hammer to be dropped safely.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: In chambering or firing a round, the actuation of the slide automatically cocks the hammer.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: By using the decocking lever, the hammer can be de-cocked without actuating the firing pin block, making it impossible to accidentally fire the weapon by using the decocking lever.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Furthermore, using the decocking lever makes the weapon "drop safe", which means the firing pin will be blocked from striking a loaded round unless the trigger is pulled.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Pulling the trigger and slowly lowering the hammer does not make the weapon "drop safe", and can result in an accidental discharge if sufficient force is applied to the hammer.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Properly decocked, the pistol can be holstered safely and can be fired in double action mode by simply pulling the trigger.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The SIG P226 has no manual safety.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Double action trigger pressure is approximately 44 N (10 lbf).

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: Subsequent shots are fired in single action mode with a lighter trigger pressure of approximately 20 N (4.5 lbf).

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: As with other DA/SA pistols such as the HK USP and Beretta 92F, some training is required to minimize the difference in point of aim caused by the different trigger pressure between a first double action shot and subsequent single action shots.

title: SIG-Sauer P226
sentence_text: The hammer may also be manually cocked at any time by the user to fire in single action mode.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox

sentence_text: It is essentially a Beretta 92FS constructed out of stainless steel with satin finish rather than steel with blued finish.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: The Inox models (from the French "inoxidable" meaning non-oxidizing) feature the following parts made in anodized stainless steel with a light bead blasting finish rather than blued carbon steel: the barrel, the slide (including the extractor, the safety and the right-side manual safety lever), the trigger (and trigger pin), and slide stop lever.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: The older Italian-made Beretta Inoxes are usually better received than the American-made Inoxes, which feature blued controls, giving them a semi two-tone look.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
